reforms.description: Austria raised the investment tax allowance for all investment outlays incurred between February 1, 1993, and March 31, 1994, from 20% to 30%, aiming to stimulate economic activity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing investment through tax allowances, the reform is expected to boost capital formation and enhance productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The investment tax allowance was raised as part of the economic stimulus package.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in the investment tax allowance is a significant measure aimed at stimulating investment, which is crucial for economic recovery.
reforms.source_quote: The investment tax allowance, for all investment outlays incurred between 1 February 1993 and 31 March 1994, is raised from 20 to 30 percent.
